title: R Tools for Political Measures
text: polimetrics R package provides collection data functions common metrics used political science research. package focuses two central concepts policy diffusion literature: geographical diffusion ideological distance. ships curated datasets U.S. state ideology geographic structure, making self-contained toolkit scholars studying policies spread across states time. methods implemented polimetrics derived foundational work policy diffusion literature: Soule & King (2006) — American Journal Sociology doi:10.1086/499908 Berry et al. (1998) — American Journal Political Science doi:10.2307/2991759 Cruz-Aceves & Mallinson (2019) — State Local Government Review doi:10.1177/0160323X20902818 Grossback et al. (2004) — American Politics Research doi:10.1177/1532673X04263801

title: Calculating Geographical Diffusion — geog.diffuse
text: Calculating Geographical Diffusion

title: Calculating Geographical Diffusion — geog.diffuse
code: geog.diffuse(df, id, neighbors, time, status, end = FALSE, keep = FALSE)

title: Calculating Geographical Diffusion — geog.diffuse
text: df data frame read . Data frame include variable character list observation's neighbors. id grouping variable, usually states counties neighbors variable character list observation's neighbors. elements character list neighbors separated commas. time time variable, observations measured. status binary, user-defined measure status policy event state given year. 0 equates policy yet occurred year, state, 1 equates policy event already adopted year, state – value 1 exist state year adopted every year thereafter). example relies ERA ratification data Soule King (2006) <doi:10.1086/499908>, merged ideology data Berry et al. (1998) <doi:10.2307/2991759>, user include measure adoption choice. end logical (default set F). set end = T, calculate percent neighbors adopted policy year-end. Otherwise, calculate based number neighbors adopted policy year-start. keep logical (default set F). set end = T, include additional variables (number neighbors number neighbors adopted policy) updated data frame.

title: Calculating Geographical Diffusion — geog.diffuse
text: function updates data frame new variable capturing geographical diffusion score.

title: Calculating Geographical Diffusion — geog.diffuse
text: Berry, William D., Ringquist, Evan J., Fording, Richard C., Hanson, Russell L. (1998) 'Measuring Citizen Government Ideology American States, 1960-93.' American Journal Political Science 42:327-348. doi:10.2307/2991759 . Soule, Sarah ., King, Brayden G. (2006) 'Stages Policy Process Equal Rights Amendment, 1972-1982.' American Journal Sociology 111:1871-1909. doi:10.1086/499908 . function calculates percent (proportion) geographically contiguous neighbors engaged event (e.g. policy adoption) given year. function can applied unit analysis time level type event.

title: Calculating Geographical Diffusion — geog.diffuse
code: data <- Ideology_ERA  geog.diffuse(data, state, neighbors, year, era_status)

(1998), measured, state, 1960 2018. adoption binary, user-defined measure policy adoption state given year. 0 equates policy adopted year, state, 1 equates policy adopted year, state – value 1 exist state year adopted (e.g. every year thereafter). example relies ERA ratification data Soule King (2006), user include measure adoption choice.

title: Calculating Ideological Distance — ideo.dist

title: Calculating Ideological Distance — ideo.dist
text: Grossback, Lawrence J., Nicholson-Crotty, Sean, Peterson, David .M. (2004) 'Ideology Learning Policy Diffusion.' American Politics Research 32:521-545. doi:10.1177/1532673X04263801 . Cruz-Aceves, Victor D., Mallinson, Daniel J. (2019) 'Clarifying Measurement Relative Ideology Policy Diffusion Research.' State Local Government Review 51:179-186. doi:10.1177/0160323X20902818 . Berry, William D., Ringquist, Evan J., Fording, Richard C., Hanson, Russell L. (1998) 'Measuring Citizen Government Ideology American States, 1960-93.' American Journal Political Science 42:327-348. doi:10.2307/2991759 . Soule, Sarah ., King, Brayden G. (2006) 'Stages Policy Process Equal Rights Amendment, 1972-1982.' American Journal Sociology 111:1871-1909. doi:10.1086/499908 . function calculates ideological distance scores based calculation created Grossback et al. (2004) clarified Cruz-Aceves Mallinson (2019). calculation based state ideology data (year) provided Richard C. Fording (https://rcfording.com/state-ideology-data/) used Berry et al. (1998). function can applied unit analysis time level type policy adoption.
